channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China tells Rubio to behave himself in veiled warning
item.description: China’s veteran foreign minister has issued a veiled warning to America’s new secretary of state: Behave yourself.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's attempt to end birthright citizenship would overturn more than a century of precedent
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has said since his first administration that he wants to end birthright citizenship, a constitutional right for everyone born in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer And Janie Har, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's border emergency declaration comes amid relative calm after years of major turmoil
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Long stretches of silence on a Border Patrol scanner are punctuated with updates on tracking a single migrant for hours. The radio traffic sounds like a throwback to earlier times, before the United States became the largest destination for asylum-seekers in 2017.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, GREGORY BULL and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Auschwitz was liberated 80 years ago. The spotlight is on survivors as their numbers dwindle
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The world's focus will be on the remaining survivors of Nazi Germany's atrocities on Monday as world leaders and royalty join them for commemorations on the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Democratic states weigh more support for immigrants as Trump administration cracks down
item.description: As President Donald Trump tightens the nation's immigration policies, lawmakers in Democratic-led states are proposing new measures that could erect legal obstacles for federal immigration officials and help immigrants lacking legal status avoid deportation.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Indiana attorney general files lawsuit against sheriff for defying feds on illegal immigration
item.description: Indiana Attorney General Todd Rokita's office is suing the St. Joseph County Sheriff's Department in South Bend, Indiana, for refusing to comply with ICE over immigration orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas Raiders agree to hire 73-year-old Pete Carroll as their head coach, AP source says
item.description: Pete Carroll has a youthful energy that many people half his age don't possess, and the Raiders hope that enthusiasm — and, more importantly, his proven track record — translates to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man says he was behind some of the viral googly eyes on public art in Oregon
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A resident of the central Oregon city of Bend says he was the person behind some of the googly eyes that appeared on sculptures around the city in recent months and sparked a viral sensation widely covered by news outlets.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota’s abortion ban will not be enforced while the state appeals an earlier decision that found it unconstitutional, the state's highest court ruled Friday. That appeal has yet to fully play out in the state Supreme Court after a judge struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anchorage Museum among Alaskans combining to win auction for Alaska Railroad's golden spike
item.description: The golden spike that was used to complete the Alaska Railroad in 1923 will for the first time be on permanent display in Alaska after entities combined to win an action for the 14-karat gold spike Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump arrives in Los Angeles to tour wildfire damage
item.description: Prior to arriving in California Friday, Trump told reporters in North Carolina that the Federal Government would not provide aid to Los Angeles recovery efforts unless new voter ID laws were passed and changes were made to the state's water management system.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Secret Service says it visited Chicago elementary school, not ICE
item.description: Chicago Public Schools officials said in a press conference Friday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents showed up at an elementary school in the city but were denied entry. But later on Friday, the Secret Service said it visited the school, not ICE.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Florida man pleads guilty to assaulting American Airlines gate agent
item.description: A Florida man pleaded guilty in federal court after he assaulted an American Airlines gate agent at Dallas-Fort Worth International Airport, according to online court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brawl over Pokémon cards breaks out at California Costco
item.description: The fight, captured on video by X user DisguisedToast, unfolded on Jan. 16 at the Costco in Atwater Village, the Los Angeles Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Newark mayor says ICE agents raided 'without warrant,' violated Constitution
item.description: New Jersey Democratic officials slammed a workplace raid conducted by Immigration and Customs Enforcement at a seafood market in Newark on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pumps coal as answer to AI power needs but any boost could be short-lived
item.description: President Donald Trump's return to office could bring some temporary relief to the beleaguered U.S. coal industry.
item.dc:creator: Mead Gruver And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: WATCH: Democrat state senator handcuffed after refusing to show driver's license at traffic stop
item.description: Body camera footage captured Oklahoma State Sen. Regina Goodwin's "very verbal" exchange with a police officer after she failed to hand over her license after being pulled over.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump cites voter ID, water flow as conditions for LA wildfire aid
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he wanted to see two actions taken in California before he offered federal support for Los Angeles as it grapples with wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mississippi bill would pay bounty hunters to catch undocumented immigrants
item.description: A Mississippi District Attorney proposed new legislation on Wednesday to pay bounty hunters a reward for helping to deport undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What JFK assassination files are still classified? Trump’s order could bring them to light
item.description: Millions of documents related to the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy in Dallas have already been made public, but President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of still-classified files.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Homeowners in Palisades Fire area angered by bank's decision
item.description: With the devastating Palisades Fire still smoldering in California, Lisa Pelton and some of her neighbors in Mandeville Canyon received an unpleasant notice from their bank: Their home equity lines of credit were being slashed.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hearing set for Kash Patel nomination to lead FBI
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, is expected to appear before the Senate Judiciary Committee on Thursday. Patel held numerous national security roles during the first Trump administration but would be a new face at the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: FBI arrests suspect in killing of Vermont Border Patrol agent
item.description: The FBI announced the arrest of a suspect Friday in the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David "Chris" Maland near the Vermont-Canada border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman finds police-issued rifle, bulletproof vests in Hertz rental
item.description: A woman was shocked after she opened the trunk of her rental car to find a police-issued assault rifle.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger looks to dodge death penalty with page from 'cult mom' Lori Vallow's playbook
item.description: As Idaho student murders suspect Bryan Kohberger's trial approaches, his defense is drawing comparisons to another high-profile case that rocked the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Carolina drunk driver arrested in December had been deported 3 times: affidavit
item.description: An illegal immigrant arrested for drunk driving in South Carolina has a lengthy criminal history in the United States and had been deported three times in less than five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Illegal immigrant in Texas wanted for allegedly raping child in Mexico repatriated back to native country
item.description: An illegal immigrant wanted in Mexico for allegedly raping a child was repatriated back to his country after entering the U.S. four times illegally since 2002.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, ABC 4

item.title: California will spend $2.5 billion to help the Los Angeles area recover from wildfires
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California will spend $2.5 billion to help the Los Angeles area recover from recent deadly wildfires under a relief package signed Thursday by Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4
